_sample_meta.note: Sample analysis output for the Aldi/Season's Choice 'Bananas Covered in Dark Chocolate and Peanut Butter' label image in this folder. Replace this file with your own analyzer output to customize the demo.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Cocoa Powder
ingredients_analyzed.classification: Deforestation & Labor Concern — West African Cocoa
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Cocoa production is a primary driver of forest loss in Ivory Coast and Ghana, which together supply ~60% of the world's cocoa. The industry also has documented child-labor concerns. Without explicit certification (Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance, Cocoa Horizons), origin cannot be assumed responsible. Carbon footprint ~19 kg CO₂e/kg.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Certified Fair Trade or Rainforest Alliance cocoa; brands with disclosed supply chains and deforestation-free commitments.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Cocoa Mass
ingredients_analyzed.classification: Deforestation & Labor Concern — West African Cocoa
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Same supply-chain concern as cocoa powder. Cocoa mass is unprocessed cocoa bean paste, the most direct indicator of cocoa volume in the product.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Full Cream Milk Powder
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Powdered whole milk requires both dairy production (~3 kg CO₂e/kg of milk) and energy-intensive spray-drying. Comparable to cheese in carbon intensity per kg of finished product (~21 kg CO₂e/kg). Methane from enteric fermentation is the dominant contributor.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Plant-based powder substitutes (oat, coconut, soy) have meaningfully lower carbon intensity, though formulation impacts vary.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Coconut production has lower deforestation pressure than palm oil but is concentrated in Philippines, Indonesia, and India. Smallholder labor conditions are highly variable; biodiversity impact is less severe than palm. No mandatory certification system equivalent to RSPO.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Domestically produced oils (sunflower, canola) have lower transport carbon; fair-trade coconut where available.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Whey
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Whey is a dairy byproduct; using it reduces waste, but the underlying dairy production carries the same carbon and methane impact as other dairy ingredients.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Skim Milk Powder
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Slightly lower carbon footprint than full cream milk powder due to fat removal, but still meaningfully impactful.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: See full cream milk powder note.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Soybean Oil
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Soybean expansion has driven Amazon and Cerrado deforestation in Brazil; the 2006 Soy Moratorium has significantly reduced direct Amazon clearing. US-grown soy has lower deforestation linkage. Origin not labeled.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Certified deforestation-free soy or domestically sourced oils.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Vanilla (Natural Flavor)
ingredients_analyzed.concern: True vanilla from Madagascar faces climate and cyclone-related supply pressure with documented labor concerns. 'Natural Flavor' may also indicate synthetic vanillin from lignin or guaiacol, which has lower agricultural footprint but different chemistry.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Fair-trade Madagascar vanilla or vanilla beans with disclosed origin.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Banana
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Banana production has pesticide and worker-condition concerns in some growing regions; Fair Trade options widely available.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Peanut (in Peanut Butter)
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Peanuts are nitrogen-fixing and have modest water and carbon footprints relative to other protein sources.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Sugar
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Sugarcane has region-dependent labor and biodiversity concerns but moderate carbon footprint.
ingredients_analyzed.alternative: Certified Bonsucro or Fair Trade sugar.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Soy Lecithin
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Soy lecithin is a byproduct of soybean oil refining; very small contribution.

ingredients_analyzed.name: Cocoa Butter
ingredients_analyzed.classification: Same supply chain as cocoa powder
ingredients_analyzed.concern: Cocoa butter shares the West African deforestation and labor concerns of other cocoa products.

summary: Environmental impact is dominated by cocoa (deforestation and labor risk in West Africa, ~19 kg CO₂e/kg) and three dairy ingredients (carbon-intensive at ~21 kg CO₂e/kg for cheese-equivalent dairy). No reef-toxic UV filters, microplastics, palm oil, or overfished species — this is a food product so the relevant axes are agricultural footprint and supply-chain sourcing rather than aquatic toxicity. No third-party certifications are indicated on the visible label.
key_concerns: Cocoa is the highest-impact ingredient: drives West African deforestation and has documented child-labor issues without certification.
Three dairy ingredients (full cream milk powder, skim milk powder, whey) substantially elevate the carbon footprint of a relatively small (30 g) serving.
No certification markings (Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance, RSPO-equivalent) are listed on the visible portion of the label.
recommendation: If sustainability is a priority, prefer chocolate products with explicit Fair Trade, Rainforest Alliance, or 'deforestation-free' certification on the package. For frequent consumption, consider lower-dairy chocolate variants or plant-based alternatives. Aldi's product lines often have parallel certified options at similar price points.
